abstract:
Human resources are the cornerstone of any successful organization, serving as the essential catalyst that transforms other assets into tangible results. Even the most advanced technology or comprehensive information systems remain stagnant if the personnel managing them lack the necessary qualifications or the ability to translate data into strategic progress. Consequently, Human Resource Management (HRM) is vital for navigating the complex interplay between individual capabilities and organizational objectives.

This paper explores the foundational frameworks of HRM, which can be categorized into administrative and operational functions. The administrative dimension encompasses the core management cycle of planning, organizing, directing, and controlling. Complementing these are six key operational functions: procurement, development, compensation, integration, maintenance, and separation. A significant focus of this study is the critical role of job analysis within the planning phase. By systematically examining job responsibilities and required competencies, organizations can ensure that the right talent is matched to the right positions, ultimately fostering an efficient and effective workplace.
